verse_specific_anchors: nitya-jātam / nityam mṛtam (constantly born / constantly dies)
tathāpi (even then) — the conditional pivot specific to 2.26
the materialist concession that distinguishes 2.26 from the imperishable verses 2.20-2.25
verdict_reason: The engineering analog is anchored on the concessive structure of atha ca... tathāpi, which is specific to 2.26. The phrase 'even on the secular premise that nothing transcends' directly mirrors Krishna's hypothetical concession of the materialist premise. This would NOT apply to 2.20-2.25 (which assert the imperishable, not concede the materialist), nor to 2.27 (which closes the conditional rather than opens it), nor to 2.28 (which is a third independent argument from beings' three-phase structure). The verse-specific anchor is the conditional-concessive shape, not 'systems die' as a generic claim.
